name: Laptop Refresh Fulfillment Workflow
serviceNowSurface: Service Catalog item plus Flow Designer fulfillment subflow; Now Assist Catalog Search at the entry surface
trigger: sc_request created against the laptop refresh catalog item
businessOutcome: Move from incident-as-request to a clean catalog flow: collect requirements through dependent variables, route fulfillment with explicit dependencies, and meet SLAs without manual hand-offs.
steps: Validate variable completeness and reject submission if any required dependent variable is missing.
Resolve laptop SKU based on role and store size via UI Policy-derived rules.
Create the hardware allocation Catalog Task assigned to inventory.
On allocation completion, create the imaging Catalog Task assigned to image team.
On imaging completion, create the shipping Catalog Task assigned to the regional logistics partner.
Apply item-specific SLA per task; notify requester at each transition.
Close the request and write structured closure data including SKU and lead time.
approvalPoints: Fulfillment lead approval on out-of-policy peripheral combinations
Catalog owner approval before re-publishing after a variable change
failureStates: Required variable missing: form blocks submission with a UI Policy message.
Imaging team SLA breach: Catalog Task escalates and a notification is sent to fulfillment lead.
Logistics partner returns failure: shipping Catalog Task reopens and notifies requester.
auditNotes: Update set retained with item and subflow.
ATF suite results archived per release.
SLA breach data tracked in Performance Analytics.
tests: Submission with all required variables produces three Catalog Tasks in correct order.
Submission missing department is blocked at the form.
Power-user SKU triggers manager approval before allocation.
Logistics failure reopens the shipping task without losing prior task data.
ATF suite runs green before merge.
